advantages: Removing or capping clear errors improves model stability and prevents a handful of bad rows from dominating metrics and coefficients.
IQR-based rules are distribution-agnostic and robust to moderate skew without assuming normality.
Clipping (winsorizing) retains sample size while bounding influence—useful when every row matters for rare classes.
Robust models and losses let you keep legitimate extremes in the data while reducing their leverage on training.
disadvantages: Aggressive removal can delete rare but informative events (fraud, failures) and hurt recall on tail behavior.
Global thresholds ignore context: the same amount may be normal for one customer and anomalous for another.
Z-scores and mean-based rules are not robust: outliers can mask themselves by shifting μ and σ.
Treatment choices leak information if bounds are fit on the full dataset before train/validation/test splits.
